Just now

github copilot vs cursor vs claude codeYou’ve heard the term. Maybe you’ve tried it. Here’s how to actually set up a vibe coding workflow that doesn’t collapse the first time you ship something real.
I want to start with something that most vibe coding tutorials skip: most people who try vibe coding for the first time get the wrong mental model and then wonder why it doesn’t work as advertised.
They treat it like autocomplete on steroids. They fire off a prompt, skim the output, paste it in, move on. And then three weeks later they have a codebase that works in demo and breaks in production, and they blame the AI.
The mental model that actually works: vibe coding is AI-assisted development where you’re the decision-maker and the AI is doing the implementation. You still own the outcome. The AI just makes implementation dramatically faster. Get that relationship right first and the rest of the setup is just mechanics.
Step 1: Pick your tool
You don’t need to spend weeks evaluating options. Here’s the short version of what each main tool is good at, so you can pick one and start.

• Claude Code (terminal-based): best for multi-file tasks, investigation, and anything where you want the AI to understand your whole project. Asks clarifying questions…
